metadata.description: Phase-transition database for elemental systems.  For each element the ordered phase sequence (solid polymorphs → liquid → gas) is listed with cumulative enthalpies and entropies relative to the DFT ground state (H = 0, S = 0).  Only solid polymorphs possessing BOTH a DFT energy (from Materials Project) AND a known transition temperature (from literature) are retained.  Liquid and gas phases are always included.

conventions.reference_state: The DFT ground state (lowest T = 0 K energy per atom from Materials Project) defines H = 0, S = 0 for each element — even when it differs from the experimentally observed ground state.
conventions.cumulative_enthalpy: H(phase) = DFT enthalpy above GS for solid polymorphs; H(liquid) = H(last solid) + H_fusion; H(gas) = H(liquid) + H_vaporization.
conventions.cumulative_entropy: S is accumulated stepwise: at each transition i, ΔS_i = ΔH_i / T_i, where ΔH_i = H_i − H_{i−1} and T_i is the transition temperature.  S(phase) = Σ ΔS_j for all j ≤ i.
conventions.enthalpy_ordering_filter: Among all solid polymorphs with known transition temperatures, the largest subset maintaining strictly increasing enthalpy with increasing temperature is retained.  A longest-increasing-subsequence (LIS) algorithm selects the maximum number of phases; ties are broken by preferring earlier (lower-T) phases.
conventions.no_heat_capacity: No Cp integration is performed. Enthalpy is treated as a step function that changes only at phase transition temperatures.
conventions.null_values: null indicates data not available from the queried sources.

sources.name: Materials Project
sources.description: GGA/GGA+U (PBE) DFT total energies. Polymorph enthalpies computed as (E_phase − E_GS) × 96 485.33212 J/mol.

sources.name: CRC Handbook of Chemistry and Physics, 96th ed. (2015)
sources.description: Fusion temperatures and enthalpies for 71 elements (primary source).

sources.name: periodictable.com / Wolfram ElementData
sources.description: Boiling points and heats of vaporization. Fallback for fusion data where CRC values are unavailable.

sources.name: Young (1991), Dinsdale/SGTE (1991), Massalski (1990)
sources.description: Solid–solid phase transition temperatures at 1 atm.

reference_state.note: DFT ground state (lowest T = 0 K energy per atom). All cumulative enthalpies and entropies are relative to this phase.

notes: DFT ground state is ω-Ti (P6/mmm, SG 191), a high-pressure phase. Used as 0 K reference per convention. α-Ti (hcp, SG 194) is the experimentally observed 0 K / 1 atm ground state but is excluded here because it lacks an assigned transition temperature in this framework.

metadata.enthalpy_source: DFT (Materials Project, GGA/GGA+U PBE)
Experimental (SGTE): Dinsdale (1991). Replaces DFT value of 3848.85 J/mol.
metadata.transition_temperature_source: Young, D.A., 'Phase Diagrams of the Elements', University of California Press, Berkeley (1991); Dinsdale, A.T., 'SGTE data for pure elements', Calphad 15(4), 317-425 (1991); Massalski, T.B. (ed.), 'Binary Alloy Phase Diagrams', 2nd ed., ASM International (1990).

metadata.enthalpy_source: Experimental (SGTE): Dinsdale (1991) — cumul. α→β→γ→δ. Replaces DFT value of 35673.08 J/mol.
metadata.transition_temperature_source: Young, D.A., 'Phase Diagrams of the Elements', University of California Press, Berkeley (1991); Dinsdale, A.T., 'SGTE data for pure elements', Calphad 15(4), 317-425 (1991); Massalski, T.B. (ed.), 'Binary Alloy Phase Diagrams', 2nd ed., ASM International (1990).

metadata.enthalpy_source: CRC Handbook of Chemistry and Physics, 96th ed. (2015) — enthalpy of fusion
metadata.transition_temperature_source: CRC Handbook of Chemistry and Physics, 96th ed. (2015)

metadata.enthalpy_source: periodictable.com (Wolfram Research ElementData) — enthalpy of vaporization

notes: Mn has four allotropes at 1 atm. γ-Mn (fcc, SG 225, 1368–1406 K) is absent from Materials Project and therefore excluded. Solid-solid transition enthalpies are from SGTE (Dinsdale 1991) because DFT greatly overestimates the α→δ energy difference.

Fe.name: Iron

reference_state.note: DFT ground state (lowest T = 0 K energy per atom). All cumulative enthalpies and entropies are relative to this phase.

metadata.enthalpy_source: DFT (Materials Project, GGA/GGA+U PBE)
Experimental (SGTE): SGTE (Dinsdale 1991) incl. magnetic model. Replaces DFT value of 14315.0 J/mol.
metadata.transition_temperature_source: Young, D.A., 'Phase Diagrams of the Elements', University of California Press, Berkeley (1991); Dinsdale, A.T., 'SGTE data for pure elements', Calphad 15(4), 317-425 (1991); Massalski, T.B. (ed.), 'Binary Alloy Phase Diagrams', 2nd ed., ASM International (1990).

metadata.enthalpy_source: DFT (Materials Project, GGA/GGA+U PBE)
metadata.transition_temperature_source: Massalski, T.B. (ed.), 'Binary Alloy Phase Diagrams', 2nd ed., ASM International (1990). γ-Fe → δ-Fe at 1667 K.

metadata.enthalpy_source: CRC Handbook of Chemistry and Physics, 96th ed. (2015) — enthalpy of fusion
metadata.transition_temperature_source: CRC Handbook of Chemistry and Physics, 96th ed. (2015)

metadata.enthalpy_source: periodictable.com (Wolfram Research ElementData) — enthalpy of vaporization

notes: Fe has a re-entrant bcc phase: α-Fe (bcc, 0–1185 K) → γ-Fe (fcc, 1185–1667 K) → δ-Fe (bcc, 1667 K – mp). δ-Fe is injected as a virtual polymorph since α-Fe and δ-Fe share the same space group (Im-3m, SG 229) in Materials Project. Transition enthalpies are from the SGTE magnetic + lattice model (Dinsdale 1991) because GGA DFT greatly overestimates the α→γ energy difference for magnetic Fe.

excluded_polymorphs.reason: Excluded by enthalpy-ordering (LIS) filter — omitted to maximise the number of solid phases with H increasing monotonically with T.  (H_rel = 76309.1 J/mol at T = 395.0 K.)
Excluded by enthalpy-ordering (LIS) filter — omitted to maximise the number of solid phases with H increasing monotonically with T.  (H_rel = 26747.4 J/mol at T = 588.0 K.)

notes: DFT ground state is P6₃/m (SG 176), which does not correspond to any of the six standard 1 atm Pu allotropes. Plutonium phase relations are poorly described by standard GGA DFT due to strong electronic-correlation effects. The enthalpy-ordering (LIS) filter retains γ-Pu and ε-Pu; α-Pu (no transition T), β-Pu, and δ-Pu are excluded as DFT energy outliers.
